competitive_position.summary: Tradogram is the incumbent competitor and primary threat. Thompson Pipe is dissatisfied with Tradogram's customer-led integration approach (60-page spec they could not execute without external help), which is Zycus's differentiation wedge: full-service integration team and implementation support. Coupa was named on 2026-05-06 as a planned evaluation target but engagement status is unknown as of 2026-06-29. Procurement Express named in prior memory but not mentioned on any call. Competitive positioning is strong on integration and implementation support but not activated: no competitive review, no EB engagement to validate differentiation, no multi-threading to IT or finance stakeholders who would validate integration value. Risk: if Coupa engages and Zycus has not locked champion and Economic Buyer, Coupa brand recognition may overtake Zycus.

competitors.name: Tradogram
competitors.quote: Tradogram required Thompson Pipe to lead integration scoping with a 60-page specification document, but Thompson Pipe's internal team could not define the integration points without external help, which Tradogram did not provide.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Carolyn stated she planned to evaluate Coupa as part of quarterly vendor benchmarking, but as of 2026-05-06 had not yet begun looking at Coupa.

competitors.name: Do Nothing
competitors.quote: Carolyn stated on 2026-05-06 that this is an exploratory evaluation with no decision timeline, conducting quarterly benchmarking continuously.

champion_strength.source: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole; Avoma calls 2026-05-06, 2026-06-10, 2026-06-12, 2026-06-16; SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-29
champion_strength.summary: Carolyn Hartzell is the developing champion: owns the evaluation, articulated pain, drove calls, and committed to building the business case. However, her champion strength is weak. She has no demonstrated access to the Economic Buyer (CFO engagement is deferred, not active), no evidence of internal advocacy beyond her own evaluation, no multi-threading to IT or finance stakeholders, and her engagement has cooled (13 days since last touch, buyer deliverables pending). She is an evaluator, not a coach or internal advocate with power to drive the deal.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-05-06, Carolyn Hartzell: deferred budget discussion
SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-16, 2026-06-29: business case 'for Carolyn to present to CFO'
Salesforce OpportunityContactRole query: only Carolyn Hartzell (Evaluator) present, no EB or Executive Sponsor role
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer (CFO or C-level sponsor with budget authority) has not been identified or engaged on any call. Carolyn Hartzell stated on the 2026-05-06 discovery call that she would need to present a business case to the CFO for budget approval, but deferred budget and ROI discussion until serious vendor selection. No CFO name, title, or contact role exists in Salesforce, and no executive participated in the discovery (2026-05-06), technical deep-dive (2026-06-10), demo (2026-06-12), or follow-up demo (2026-06-16) calls. The Next Step log (2026-06-16, 2026-06-29) references building a business case 'for Carolyn to present to CFO', confirming Carolyn is the messenger, not the Economic Buyer. Without CFO engagement, budget validation and deal approval remain unconfirmed, which is a critical gap for a $120k deal at 94 days to close.

recommended_moves: Re-engage Carolyn with a direct call to unblock the intake form and buyer deliverables (specific questions, approval workflow requirements, supplier access expectations). If Carolyn remains non-responsive, escalate to executive connect: have the deal owner's manager reach out to Carolyn or request introduction to the CFO to validate urgency and budget.

Secure CFO introduction and schedule business case review call with Carolyn and the CFO within 14 days. Position the call as ROI validation and budget alignment, not a handoff. Use the call to map the CFO's decision timeline, budget authority, and procurement software replacement priority.

Multi-thread into IT: request Carolyn introduce Zycus to the relevant stakeholder lead responsible for Sage and Crexendo ERP integrations. Schedule technical scoping session to demonstrate Zycus full-service integration approach (the core differentiator against Tradogram) and scope implementation SOW.
